title: Invalid input for "-o=uid=" option to "podman volume create" does not trigger an error
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**


Invalid input for "-o=uid=" option to "podman volume create" does not trigger an error.


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

```
[core@localhost ~]$ podman volume create --driver=local -o=uid=foobar test
test
[core@localhost ~]$ 
```


**Describe the results you received:**


The output
```
test
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

That an error message would have been shown.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I wonder if the UID and GID options work when valid input is provided?

When I run

```
[core@localhost ~]$ podman volume create --driver=local -o=uid=3000,gid=3000 test10
test10
[core@localhost ~]$ podman volume create --driver=local  test11
test11
[core@localhost ~]$
```
I don't see any difference in the ownership of the  created directories

```
[core@localhost ~]$ id
uid=1000(core) gid=1000(core) groups=1000(core),4(adm),10(wheel),16(sudo),190(systemd-journal) context=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023
[core@localhost ]$ ls -ld /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test10
drwx------. 3 core core 19 May 19 09:52 /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test10
[core@localhost ~]$ ls -ld /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test10/_data/
drwxr-xr-x. 2 core core 6 May 19 09:52 /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test10/_data/
[core@localhost ~]$ ls -ld /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test11
drwx------. 3 core core 19 May 19 09:52 /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test11
[core@localhost ~]$ ls -ld /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test11/_data/
drwxr-xr-x. 2 core core 6 May 19 09:52 /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test11/_data/
[core@localhost ~]$ 
```

Is that expected?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Mon May  9 12:46:09 2022

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I booted up the VM _fedora-coreos-37.20220514.91.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2_

comments.body:
It works when I add __--opt device=tmpfs --opt type=tmpfs__
```
[core@localhost ~]$ podman volume create --opt device=tmpfs --opt type=tmpfs --opt o=uid=hello,gid=hello  test16
Error: cannot convert UID hello to integer: strconv.Atoi: parsing "hello": invalid syntax
[core@localhost ~]$
```

```
[core@localhost ~]$ podman volume create --opt device=tmpfs --opt type=tmpfs --opt o=uid=3000,gid=3000   test14
test14
[core@localhost ~]$ podman volume create --opt device=tmpfs --opt type=tmpfs test15
test15
[core@localhost ~]$ ls -ld /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test14
drwx------. 3 102999 102999 19 May 19 13:01 /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test14
[core@localhost ~]$ ls -ld /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test15
drwx------. 3 core core 19 May 19 13:01 /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test15
[core@localhost ~]$ 
```

I guess it works as expected.
